01_initial_request.input_data: Retrospective clinical data from 17 patients with metastatic HER2+ cancer on long-term anti-HER2 therapy
01_initial_request.user_intent: To investigate the incidence and risk factors of late Cancer therapy-related cardiac dysfunction (CTRCD) associated with long-term anti-HER2 therapy
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction (LVEF) recovery rate and normalization

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Long-term anti-HER2 therapy in metastatic HER2+ cancers is increasing, but data about incidence and risk factors for late CTRCD are missing. [Gap] No data exists on cardiac dysfunction occurring after at least 5 years of continuous anti-HER2 therapy. [Decision] To investigate late CTRCD, chose to use retrospective clinical database review to perform patient cohort identification on patients referred to Cardio-Oncology service, expecting to obtain eligible patient population.

parameters.inclusion_criteria: CTRCD after at least 5 years of continuous anti-HER2 therapy

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Seventeen patients identified fulfilling inclusion criteria

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Patient cohort has been identified with 17 patients. [Gap] Demographic and clinical characteristics of the cohort are unknown. [Decision] To characterize the cohort, chose to use medical record review to perform baseline data collection on patient demographics and clinical history, expecting to obtain comprehensive patient profiles.

parameters.data_collected: age, sex, cancer type, treatment history, cardiovascular risk factors, baseline echocardiogram

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 16 women (94%) with metastatic HER2+ breast cancer, 1 man with metastatic HER2+ gastric adenocarcinoma; median age 58 years (IQR 54-61)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Patient cohort characterized with demographics. [Gap] CTRCD severity needs to be classified according to standardized definitions. [Decision] To categorize CTRCD severity, chose to use ICOS classification system to perform severity assessment on CTRCD cases, expecting to obtain standardized severity categories.

parameters.alternative_criteria: ≥10% reduction in LVEF and/or below institutional lower limit of normal

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 9 patients (53%) asymptomatic mild, 5 patients (29%) asymptomatic moderate, 1 patient (6%) symptomatic moderate, 2 patients (12%) symptomatic severe CTRCD

references.citation: Herrmann J, Lenihan D, Armenian S, et al. Defining cardiovascular toxicities of cancer therapies: an International Cardio-Oncology Society (IC-OS) consensus statement. Eur Heart J. January 2022;43(4):280–99.
references.relevance: Provides ICOS definitions for CTRCD severity classification

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CTRCD severity has been classified. [Gap] Cardiac function metrics at time of CTRCD diagnosis need to be quantified. [Decision] To measure cardiac dysfunction, chose to use echocardiography to perform LVEF and GLS assessment on patients at CTRCD diagnosis, expecting to obtain quantitative cardiac function measurements.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Lowest median LVEF 49% (IQR 45-55), median GLS -15.4% (IQR -14.9 to -16.3)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cardiac dysfunction quantified with LVEF and GLS measurements. [Gap] Cardioprotective intervention needed to manage CTRCD. [Decision] To manage cardiac dysfunction, chose to use heart failure medical therapy to perform cardioprotective treatment on patients, expecting to obtain cardiac function improvement.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 16 patients started on heart failure therapy in line with guidelines

references.citation: Lyon AR, López-Fernández T, Couch LS, et al. ESC guidelines on cardio-oncology. Eur Heart J. 2022;43(41):4229–361.
references.relevance: Provides ESC guidelines for cardio-oncology management

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Heart failure therapy initiated in 16/17 patients. [Gap] Anti-HER2 therapy management strategy needs to be determined. [Decision] To balance cancer treatment and cardiac safety, chose to use clinical decision-making to perform anti-HER2 therapy continuation or interruption on patients, expecting to obtain treatment continuation with cardiac safety.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: All patients continued or restarted anti-HER2 therapy after brief interruption; 1 patient experienced cancer progression during interruption

references.citation: Leong DP, Cosman T, Alhussein MM, et al. Safety of Continuing Trastuzumab despite mild cardiotoxicity: a phase I Trial. JACC CardioOncol. 2019;1(1):1–10.
references.relevance: Supports continuation of anti-HER2 therapy under cardioprotective medication

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Anti-HER2 therapy management completed with all patients continuing treatment. [Gap] Cardiac function recovery needs to be assessed during follow-up. [Decision] To evaluate treatment efficacy, chose to use follow-up echocardiography to perform LVEF recovery assessment on patients during follow-up, expecting to obtain LVEF recovery and normalization data.

parameters.recovery_assessment: complete or partial recovery of systolic function

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Median LVEF at last follow-up 59% (IQR 51-62); complete recovery 11/17 (65%), partial recovery 3/17 (18%); recovery occurred within median 2 months (IQR 1.5-2.9)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] LVEF recovery assessed with majority showing improvement. [Gap] Contributing factors to CTRCD development need to be identified. [Decision] To understand risk factors, chose to use clinical analysis to perform second hit identification on CTRCD cases, expecting to obtain contributing factor profile.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Second cardiac insult identified in 9 cases (53%); half of patients had no concomitant event suggesting cumulative anti-HER2 effect

references.citation: Ito M, Horimoto Y, Sasaki R, et al. Cardiotoxicity after additional administration of Pertuzumab following long-term trastuzumab: report of 2 cases. Case Rep Oncol. 2021;14(1):62–6.
references.relevance: Describes late LVEF decline following addition of Pertuzumab to ongoing trastuzumab

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Retrospective clinical cohort analysis with echocardiographic follow-up

patient_cohort_size.interpretation: Total patients with late CTRCD after ≥5 years anti-HER2 therapy

time_to_CTRCD.interpretation: Median time on anti-HER2 therapy before CTRCD occurrence (IQR 5.3-9.0)

lowest_LVEF.interpretation: Median lowest LVEF at CTRCD diagnosis (IQR 45-55)

LVEF_recovery_rate.interpretation: Combined complete (65%) and partial (18%) recovery rate

LVEF_normalization.interpretation: 16/17 patients normalized LVEF at follow-up (median 59%, IQR 51-62)

recovery_time.interpretation: Median time to LV systolic function recovery (IQR 1.5-2.9)

second_hit_prevalence.interpretation: Proportion of patients with second cardiac insult contributing to CTRCD

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved: Study successfully demonstrated that CTRCD can occur after many years of stability on anti-HER2 therapy (median 6.5 years), identified risk factors including second cardiac insults (53% of cases), and quantified LVEF recovery rate (83% combined complete/partial) with 94% normalization at follow-up. Late anti-HER2-related cardiac dysfunction remains a limited and reversible condition when managed properly with cardioprotective therapy, allowing continuation of anti-HER2 treatment.
